name: Cross-platform and screenshot-first alternatives
summary: Makerlapse uses screenshot bursts stitched to video (Electron cross-platform), emphasizing light files and optional soundtrack — Mac build historically weak on Catalina+. Lapse targets Mac+Windows menubar with direct timelapse encode. Cross-platform expands TAM but increases capture complexity (Windows Graphics Capture, Linux PipeWire) versus a focused macOS SCK product.
key_findings: Makerlapse roadmap included merge-while-recording; Community sharing was marketed coming soon on makerlapse.netlify.app; Screenshot-first avoids huge intermediate video but needs robust stitch pipeline; Windows timelapse menubar exists via Lapse per BetaList
implications_for_trylapse: If Claude share emphasized cross-platform, phase 2 product after macOS PMF; Screenshot pipeline is valid MVP path for crash safety (segmented JPEG + ffmpeg stitch); Soundtrack is nice-to-have not wedge
strengths: Cross-platform reach; Simpler mental model (photos → video)
weaknesses: Makerlapse Mac stability called out in README; Less integrated with macOS permissions UX; Competing with Lapse on Windows without differentiation is hard
